flashcards.definition: Circle of radius 1 centered at the origin; point at angle θ is (cos θ, sin θ).
0°, 30°, 45°, 60°, 90° and their multiples; have exact trig values using √2 and √3.
All trig positive in Q I; Sine in Q II; Tangent in Q III; Cosine in Q IV.
The acute angle between the terminal side and the x-axis.
x = cos θ, y = sin θ for any angle θ.
